mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks. Key trade-offs include the need for a Kubernetes environment and the complexity of initial setup.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the Kagenti CLI tool to manage deployments and configurations.

```bash

curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

```

> This command downloads the CLI tool and makes it executable.
commands: curl -Lo kagenti-cli https://github.com/kagenti/kagenti/releases/download/v0.5.1/kagenti-cli-linux-amd64 && chmod +x kagenti-cli && sudo mv kagenti-cli /usr/local/bin/

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Set up a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create kagenti-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a cluster named 'kagenti-cluster' with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to connect to the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command lists all nodes and their statuses.

steps.title: Install Kagenti
steps.description:
Deploy Kagenti on your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti --create-namespace

```

> Add the Kagenti Helm repository.
> Install Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling and monitoring for your Kagenti deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/autoscale.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This will remove the Kagenti deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstall the Kagenti release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade the deployment, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --version 0.5.1 --namespace kagenti

kubectl get pods -n kagenti

```

> Backup current configuration.
> Upgrade to the latest version.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kubernetes cluster unreachable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig may not be set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials kagenti-cluster --region=us-central1' to update kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install chart: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm chart may not be available or the version is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure the correct Helm repo is added and the version is specified correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may have configuration issues or missing dependencies.

**Fix:**
Check pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU or memory'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in the cluster.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new AI agent protocols and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n kagenti' showing all pods in 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding.
